vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1001

plan.title: fix(release_publish): REST `/releases/tags/{tag}` 404s on draft releases (post-#961 regression)

narratives.Description: The REST migration in #961 routed scripts/release_publish.py through GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/tags/{tag}, which returns 404 for draft releases and breaks task release:publish on a freshly cut draft. The fix resolves the release by id (or by scanning the releases list for the matching tag_name) so the publish step flips draft=false without the manual gh release edit recovery used during the v0.26.2 cut.
narratives.Overview:
## Problem

During the v0.26.2 release on 2026-05-08, `task release:publish -- 0.26.2` failed with:

```
[publish] View v0.26.2 on deftai/directive... FAIL (release v0.26.2 not found on deftai/directive: gh: Not Found (HTTP 404))
```

This blocked the canonical Phase 6 publish branch in `skills/deft-directive-release/SKILL.md`.

## Root cause

`scripts/release_publish.py` was migrated to REST in #961 to route around the GraphQL bucket. The REST `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/tags/{tag}` endpoint **only finds published releases** by tag — it returns 404 for drafts, even though the draft has a `tag_name` field.

GitHub's draft release URL format confirms this asymmetry: `https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/releases/tag/untagged-<hash>` (note the `untagged-` prefix on drafts vs `v<version>` on published).

The legacy `gh release view <tag>` CLI surface uses GraphQL and does find drafts. The REST migration in #961 inadvertently regressed this case.

## Reproduction

1. `task release -- <version>` → lands draft
2. Verify draft via `gh release view v<version>` (works — GraphQL)
3. `task release:publish -- <version>` → 404 (REST `/releases/tags/{tag}` doesn't find drafts)

## Workaround used during v0.26.2 cut

```
gh release edit v0.26.2 --repo deftai/directive --draft=false
```

Direct `gh release edit` is GraphQL-backed and works on drafts. This is the manual recovery the operator used when `release_publish.py` failed.

## Suggested fix

Three options (ordered by preference):

1. **Fetch by ID, not by tag.** `release_publish.py` already calls `gh release view` (or could) to get the release ID, then PATCH `/releases/{id}` with `draft=false`. The by-ID REST endpoint works for both drafts and published releases.
2. **List all releases and match `tag_name`.** `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases` returns drafts in the list; iterate to find the matching `tag_name`. Slower (paginated) but works.
3. **Document the gap** and route this specific call through `gh release edit --draft=false` (GraphQL) as a documented exception, similar to the `gh pr ready` and `gh pr review --approve` exceptions already documented in `scripts/gh_rest.py` module docstring.

Option 1 is the cleanest. Option 3 is the smallest patch.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] AC-1: `task release:publish -- <version>` succeeds against a draft release without falling back to manual `gh release edit`.
- [ ] AC-2: New regression test in `tests/cli/test_release_publish.py` covering the draft-tag-lookup case (REST 404 -> draft-aware fallback).
- [ ] AC-3: `scripts/gh_rest.py` module docstring updated if option 3 is taken (route documentation).
- [ ] AC-4: CHANGELOG entry under `[Unreleased]` / `### Fixed`.

## Related

- #961 — `gh_rest.py` REST helpers + `release_publish.py` REST refactor (the regression source).
- #954 — REST-default rule for `gh` reads.
- #74 / #716 / #724 — `task release` / `task release:publish` / `task release:rollback` surface.
- #784 — pre-flight tag availability gate.

## Recurrence record

First occurrence: v0.26.2 release on 2026-05-08. Recovery via manual `gh release edit --draft=false`.
narratives.ImplementationPlan: Update the scripts/release_publish.py module to fetch the release by id (via gh release view or the releases list) instead of the tag-only REST endpoint, so draft releases resolve before the draft=false PATCH. Update the scripts/gh_rest.py module docstring to record the draft-aware lookup route alongside the existing GraphQL exceptions. Add a regression test in tests/cli/test_release_publish.py that simulates a REST 404 on the tag endpoint and asserts the draft-aware fallback still publishes.
narratives.UserStory: As a deft release operator, I want task release:publish to resolve a draft release by id instead of by tag, so that publishing a drafted version succeeds without a manual gh release edit fallback.

items.title: Draft releases publish without a manual gh release edit fallback.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a draft release for the target version, when task release:publish runs, then it publishes the release and never falls back to manual gh release edit.

items.title: release_publish resolves a draft by id when the tag endpoint 404s.

narrative.Acceptance: Given the REST tag endpoint returns 404 for a draft, when release_publish resolves the release, then it locates the draft by id and flips draft to false.

items.title: Regression test covers the draft-tag 404 fallback path.

narrative.Acceptance: A regression test in tests/cli/test_release_publish.py simulates the draft-tag 404 and validates that the draft-aware fallback publishes the release successfully.

items.title: Published (non-draft) releases still resolve via the by-id path.

narrative.Acceptance: Given the by-id lookup path, when a published non-draft release is requested, then task release:publish still resolves and publishes it without regression.

references.title: Issue #1001: fix(release_publish): REST `/releases/tags/{tag}` 404s on draft releases (post-#961 regression)

expected_outputs: release:publish resolves draft releases by id
regression test for the draft-tag 404 fallback
pull request fixing the #961 REST regression
